articles.title: IPHONE 18 PRO VS IPHONE FOLD: THESE CAMERA FEATURES ARE RUMORED
articles.summary: Apple's fall lineup is rumored to shrink to just iPhone 18 Pro, 18 Pro Max, and iPhone Fold, effectively giving buyers a choice between Pro or Fold, with cameras as a key differentiator. The 18 Pro models are expected to keep two camera advantages over the Fold—variable aperture on the main camera and a telephoto lens with wider aperture—while the Fold may skip telephoto entirely.

articles.title: PIXELMATOR PRO, ONE OF THE BEST IMAGE EDITING APPS, FINALLY LANDS ON THE IPAD
articles.summary: Pixelmator Pro, a highly regarded Mac image editing app and Photoshop alternative, has finally launched on iPad as part of Apple's Creator Studio bundle. The app features a layer-based editing system, advanced tools, and machine learning capabilities, such as automatic background removal, all optimized for touch and Apple Pencil input. Projects sync seamlessly between iPad and Mac devices. It requires iPadOS 26 or later with an A16/A17 Pro chip or newer.

articles.title: XBOX CLOUD GAMING'S NEW DESIGN TEASES THE FUTURE OF XBOX CONSOLE UI
articles.summary: Microsoft is testing a new web interface for Xbox Cloud Gaming that features console-like design elements, including updated navigation, animations, and a rounded aesthetic. The company describes this refreshed UI as the "foundation" for building new Xbox experiences, suggesting it may preview future Xbox console interfaces. Xbox Insiders can access the preview through the settings menu at play.xbox.com to test the improved dashboard and library features.

articles.title: SYSTEMS COLLAPSE: WHY CREATIVE EDUCATION NEEDS A BAUHAUS-SIZED RESET
articles.summary: Amid AI, climate crisis, and deep uncertainty, creative education needs radical reimagining—from exam-led silos to a Bauhaus-like, process-driven model centred on making, systems thinking, judgment, and agency. Creativity should become the foundation of learning, reconnecting education, industry, and policy to rebuild confidence, empathy, and responsibility in a world where execution matters less than meaning.

articles.title: AI IS FINALLY LEARNING TO SHUT UP
articles.summary: AI interface design is shifting away from chat-based conversations toward one-click, ambient actions that remove friction by acting directly in context while keeping humans in control. Conversation is often overhead: the most effective AI minimizes interaction, translates intuitive user intent into precise machine instructions, and reserves chat only for ambiguous, educational, or emotional tasks.

articles.title: FROM DESIGN-FIRST TO MOTION-DRIVEN: DYLAN BROUWER'S JOURNEY INTO THE NO-CODE FRONTIER
articles.summary: Dylan Brouwer is a Dutch digital designer and Webflow developer who creates expressive, animated websites using no-code tools like Webflow and GSAP. His recent portfolio showcases his ability to blend bold visuals with intuitive UX, including a complex homepage hero with a 3D-rotating display and scroll-triggered animations. He also worked on Palmer Dinnerware's website at UNCOMMON, reimagining it as an immersive digital canvas rather than a traditional e-commerce catalog.

articles.title: BUILD SOMETHING REAL
articles.summary: Imagine is an AI builder platform that lets users create functional products like apps, dashboards, and websites by simply describing their ideas in natural language.

articles.title: 3D ICON LIBRARY
articles.summary: Icoon is a 3D icon library offering AI-generated 3D icons, illustrations, and creative design assets for websites, apps, and print materials. The platform provides access to over 1,500 high-quality design assets.

articles.title: GIRAFFE, MUPPET, OR HUMAN?
articles.summary: Designing VR avatars for children works best when they strike a balance of moderate realism: clearly non-human but plausible (like an animal), avoiding both hyper-realistic humans and overly fantastical characters that can feel creepy or confusing. Research shows consistency matters most—appearance, proportions, movement (especially eyes), and distance must align—because mismatches intensify discomfort in VR, where kids' emotions and sense of reality are heightened.

articles.title: THE MOST LOVED AND LOATHED FONTS ACCORDING TO CREATIVE PROS
articles.summary: Adobe's study of 415 discussions from creative professionals revealed the most loved and loathed fonts in the design community. Lato topped the loved list for being clean and versatile, while Comic Sans predictably ranked as the most hated for its childish appearance. The study showed that "safe" fonts like Arial are often more offensive to creatives than genuinely bad fonts, and trend fatigue has made previously popular fonts like Montserrat undesirable.

articles.title: VOLKSWAGEN UX DESIGN LEAD UNVEILS ‘SECRET SAUCE'
articles.summary: Volkswagen's UX design lead, Mathias Kuhn, revealed the company's three core values for user experience design: stable, likeable, and exciting, which he calls their "secret sauce." The approach focuses on user-centricity, with customer feedback integral to design decisions, as seen in the new ID Polo cockpit, which blends modern software with retro elements. Kuhn emphasizes using AI as a conductor to seamlessly integrate various technologies while maintaining simple, intuitive interfaces that avoid tech overload.

articles.title: COMBOBOX VS. MULTISELECT VS. LISTBOX – SMART INTERFACE DESIGN PATTERNS
articles.summary: UI components like combobox, multiselect, listbox, and dual listbox handle list interactions differently based on the number of options and visibility needs.

articles.title: UX SIGNALS THAT INDICATE USERS ARE GETTING LOST
articles.summary: UX signals indicate when users become lost while navigating digital interfaces.
